H100_x2.description: 2x H100 80GB PCIe per docs/support-matrix.md — self-hosted Docker Compose default.

env: A Linux host with 2x NVIDIA H100 80GB GPUs, nvidia-container-toolkit installed, Docker + Docker Compose plugin running. Self-hosted deployment — all NIM containers (LLM, embedding, ranking, ingestion) run locally on the H100s using deploy/compose/.env. Required env: NGC_API_KEY (nvapi-) for nvcr.io pulls. cwd is ${RAG_REPO_ROOT}. Source deploy/compose/.env (NOT nvdev.env). Bring up vectordb, local NIMs (nims.yaml), ingestor server, and rag server.

expects.query: Deploy NVIDIA RAG Blueprint in self-hosted Docker Compose mode on the local H100 GPUs. Source deploy/compose/.env, then start the vector DB (vectordb.yaml), the local NIMs (nims.yaml — LLM + embedding + ranking + ingestion), the ingestor server (docker-compose-ingestor-server.yaml), and the rag server (docker-compose-rag-server.yaml). All model inference must use the local NIM containers — do NOT use cloud endpoints (integrate.api.nvidia.com).
checks: `docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^(nim-llm|nemoretriever-embedding-ms|nemoretriever-ranking-ms)' | wc -l` outputs a number greater than or equal to 3
`nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv,noheader,nounits | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'` outputs a number greater than 5000 (NIMs have loaded weights into GPU memory, total >5 GB across GPUs)

`docker exec rag-server env 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^APP_LLM_SERVERURL='` outputs a line that does NOT contain integrate.api.nvidia.com (must point at a local NIM endpoint or be unset, never the cloud endpoint)

The agent's final output claims the deployment succeeded AND lists or references the running local NIM containers (nim-llm or nemoretriever-*) — a deploy success message that omits any mention of local NIMs is NOT acceptable for self-hosted mode

expects.query: Verify the deployed self-hosted RAG stack is healthy. Hit the rag-server health endpoint, the ingestor-server health endpoint, and confirm at least one local NIM container reports a healthy status. Report each.
checks: `docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}' | grep -E '(nim-llm|nemoretriever-embedding-ms|nemoretriever-ranking-ms)' | grep -v 'Up' | wc -l` outputs 0 (every running NIM container is in 'Up' state — none Restarting / Created / Exited)
`docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}' | grep -E '(milvus-standalone|rag-server|ingestor-server)' | grep -v 'Up' | wc -l` outputs 0 (every core service container is in 'Up' state)

The agent's final output reports a per-service health verdict for rag-server, ingestor-server, and at least one local NIM, each with a clear status indicator (HTTP 200, 'Healthy', 'Up', or equivalent). A single overall summary alone is NOT sufficient — per-service breakdown is required
